homepage_url_justification: The pkgdown homepage is https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/ and is linked from DESCRIPTION.
sites_https_justification: The project homepage, source repository, and development distribution are all served over HTTPS: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr , https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/ , and https://bart-turczynski.r-universe.dev .
description_good_justification: DESCRIPTION and the project homepage succinctly describe pagerankr as an R toolkit for PageRank modelling on crawl and analytics data: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/
interact_justification: The project homepage links to installation, documentation, bug reporting, and contribution information; contribution instructions are at https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md and the issue tracker is https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents acceptable contribution requirements: tests, lintr, R CMD check, and the local and CI verification gates: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
license_location_justification: LICENSE is at the repository top level: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/LICENSE
floss_license_osi_justification: MIT is an OSI-approved open source license: https://opensource.org/license/mit
documentation_basics_justification: Basic documentation covers installation, getting started, usage (with a tutorial-style vignette), and secure use. Install/quick-start instructions and worked examples are in the README and on the pkgdown site: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/ ; the usage tutorial vignette is at https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/articles/pagerankr-usage.html ; guidance on safely handling untrusted crawl/URL/link-graph input is in SECURITY.md: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/SECURITY.md
documentation_interface_justification: Every exported function has roxygen2-generated reference documentation describing arguments and return values: https://bart-turczynski.github.io/pagerankr/reference/index.html
repo_track_justification: All source changes are tracked in git: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/commits/main
repo_interim_justification: Interim development commits are publicly available between releases: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/commits/main
repo_distributed_justification: Uses git, a distributed version control system: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr
version_unique_justification: Each release is assigned a unique DESCRIPTION version and published release/tag: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/releases
version_semver_justification: The package uses semantic-style x.y.z versioning, visible in releases and DESCRIPTION: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/releases
version_tags_justification: Releases are identified with v* git tags, as configured in CI: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/full-check.yml
release_notes_justification: NEWS.md documents user-facing changes for each release: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/NEWS.md
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date; NEWS.md records all changes: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/NEWS.md
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues is the public issue tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
report_process_justification: Users submit bug reports through the GitHub issue tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
report_responses_justification: The maintainer acknowledges and responds to bug reports in the public tracker: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement requests are accepted and responded to through GitHub Issues: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provides a publicly available searchable archive of reports and responses: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues?q=is%3Aissue
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: The vulnerability-reporting process is published in SECURITY.md: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/security/policy (source: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/SECURITY.md )
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting is the documented confidential channel, with email as a fallback: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/security/advisories/new
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging reports within seven days and coordinating a fix and disclosure: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/SECURITY.md
build_justification: The package builds with the standard R toolchain and R CMD check; the reproducible CI configuration is in https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
build_common_tools_justification: It uses standard R build tooling and GitHub Actions.
build_floss_tools_justification: R and its build toolchain are free/open-source software: https://www.r-project.org/
test_justification: The FLOSS testthat suite is public and its invocation is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md and CI: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/tree/main/tests/testthat
test_invocation_justification: Tests run via devtools::test() and as part of R CMD check; see https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
test_most_justification: Coverage is measured with covr and uploaded to Codecov by Verify: https://app.codecov.io/gh/bart-turczynski/pagerankr
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires testing and verification of changes before push: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
tests_are_added_justification: Recent major functionality is accompanied by focused testthat files in the public suite: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/tree/main/tests/testthat
tests_documented_added_justification: The contribution and verification expectations are documented in CONTRIBUTING.md: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
warnings_justification: Warnings are surfaced via R CMD check and lintr in CI: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/actions/workflows/verify.yml
warnings_fixed_justification: The pre-push gate fails on R CMD check warnings and errors.
warnings_strict_justification: Verify sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT=true so any lint fails the build, and the local check runs --as-cran: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
know_secure_design_justification: The package defensively processes untrusted URL, redirect, crawl, and link-graph data.
know_common_errors_justification: Input validation and tests cover malformed inputs, redirect loops, and graph edge cases.
crypto_password_storage_justification: pagerankr stores no passwords or credentials.
crypto_random_justification: pagerankr does not generate security-sensitive random values.
crypto_used_network_justification: pagerankr does not itself make network connections.
crypto_tls12_justification: pagerankr does not establish network connections.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: pagerankr does not establish network connections.
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: pagerankr does not establish network connections.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source and development releases are delivered over HTTPS-backed GitHub and r-universe: https://bart-turczynski.r-universe.dev
delivery_unsigned_justification: All distribution downloads are served over HTTPS (GitHub, r-universe): https://bart-turczynski.r-universe.dev
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No outstanding known vulnerabilities; SECURITY.md defines the response process and the public tracker is https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No known unfixed critical vulnerabilities exist.
static_analysis_justification: lintr runs as a full-package static analysis check in the Verify workflow: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: R CMD check plus lintr cover common defect classes on every CI build: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/actions/workflows/verify.yml
static_analysis_fixed_justification: Findings must be resolved for the verification gate to pass.
static_analysis_often_justification: lintr runs on every CI build (each push and pull request): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/verify.yml
dynamic_analysis_justification: The testthat suite runs during R CMD check on the routine Ubuntu CI and the full release matrix: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/blob/main/.github/workflows/full-check.yml
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: pagerankr contains no compiled source code; it is implemented in R.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: pagerankr's testthat expectations (expect_*) are runtime assertions exercised on every CI run: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/tree/main/tests/testthat
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Test and check failures block the verification gate.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: GitHub Actions (verify.yml) runs lint + R CMD check + coverage on every push and pull request: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/actions/workflows/verify.yml
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues provide a public forum for project discussion: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/issues
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials, secrets, or tokens are committed to the repository: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr
english_justification: Documentation and issue-tracker interaction are conducted in English (Language: en-US): https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr#readme
hardening_justification: Hardening mechanisms such as HTTP response headers apply to web applications; pagerankr is an R library.
sites_password_security_justification: pagerankr operates no site that manages user passwords.
maintained_justification: The project is actively maintained, with current development and routine verification: https://github.com/bart-turczynski/pagerankr/commits/main
